defs.name: Null
defs.docs: The null set. This never matches anything.

attributes.value: / The null set. This never matches anything.

defs.name: Empty
defs.docs: The empty string (matches exactly "").

attributes.value: / The empty string (matches exactly "").

defs.name: Except
defs.docs: Matches any single character except the listed ones.

attributes.value: / Matches any single character except the listed ones.

defs.name: Alt
defs.docs:
Alternation (also known as disjunction). Matches any of the contained
 characters, as well as any string matched by a contained regex.

attributes.value: / Alternation (also known as disjunction). Matches any of the contained
/ characters, as well as any string matched by a contained regex.

defs.name: And
defs.docs: Conjunction. Matches iff all contained regexes match.

attributes.value: / Conjunction. Matches iff all contained regexes match.

defs.name: Not
defs.docs: Negation. Matches iff the contained regex does not match.

attributes.value: / Negation. Matches iff the contained regex does not match.

defs.name: Cat
defs.docs: Concatenation. Matches iff the contained regexes match in sequence.

attributes.value: / Concatenation. Matches iff the contained regexes match in sequence.

defs.name: Kleene
defs.docs: Kleene closure. Matches zero or more repetitions of the contained regex.

attributes.value: / Kleene closure. Matches zero or more repetitions of the contained regex.

defs.name: Regex
defs.docs: A regular expression over the alphabet `T`.

attributes.value: / A regular expression over the alphabet `T`.

defs.name: ParseError
nullable
defs.docs:
Tests whether a regular expression is nullable, i.e. whether it matches
 the empty string.

defs.name: Derivatives
defs.docs: The set of some object's derivatives with respect to an alphabet `T`.

attributes.value: / The set of some object's derivatives with respect to an alphabet `T`.

defs.name: d
defs.docs:
Holds a set of pairs `(chars, derivative)`, meaning that the derivative
 with respect to any element of `chars` is `derivative`.

attributes.value: / Holds a set of pairs `(chars, derivative)`, meaning that the derivative
/ with respect to any element of `chars` is `derivative`.

defs.name: rest
defs.docs: The derivative with respect to any character not listed in `d`.

attributes.value: / The derivative with respect to any character not listed in `d`.

defs.value: pub fn map<F: FnMut(R) -> R>(Self, F) -> Derivatives<T, R>

defs.name: Differentiable
defs.docs:
A trait for types which can be differentiated with respect to an alphabet
 `T`.

defs.name: State
defs.docs: A state in a DFA.

attributes.value: / A state in a DFA.

defs.name: by_char
defs.docs: Labelled transitions.

attributes.value: / Labelled transitions.

defs.name: default
defs.docs:
The default transition (for values not in `by_char`).
 Note that `by_char` is assumed not to cover the entire alphabet (`T`).

attributes.value: / The default transition (for values not in `by_char`).

defs.name: value
defs.docs: A value associated with the state.

attributes.value: / A value associated with the state.

defs.name: Dfa
defs.docs:
A deterministic finite automaton (DFA), over the alphabet `T`.
 Each state is annotated with a value of type `V`.
 State 0 is the starting state.

attributes.value: / A deterministic finite automaton (DFA), over the alphabet `T`.
/ Each state is annotated with a value of type `V`.
/ State 0 is the starting state.

defs.name: states
defs.docs: The list of states.

attributes.value: / The list of states.

defs.name: from_derivatives
defs.value:
pub fn from_derivatives(Vec<V>) -> (Dfa<T, V>, BTreeMap<V, u32>) where T: Ord,
V: Differentiable<T> + Normalize + Ord + Clone
defs.docs:
Construct a DFA from a list of differentiable objects.
 The elements of `initial` form the first states of the DFA.
 Returns the DFA, together with a mapping from derivatives to state numbers.

attributes.value: / Construct a DFA from a list of differentiable objects.
/ The elements of `initial` form the first states of the DFA.
/ Returns the DFA, together with a mapping from derivatives to state numbers.

defs.name: map
defs.value: pub fn map<U, F>(Self, F) -> Dfa<T, U> where F: FnMut(V) -> U
defs.docs: Apply a function to each state's value.

attributes.value: / Apply a function to each state's value.

defs.name: reverse
defs.value:
pub fn reverse(&Self) -> Vec<(BTreeMap<&T, BTreeSet<usize>>, BTreeSet<usize>)>
where T: Ord
defs.docs: Find the reverse transitions from each state in the DFA.

attributes.value: / Find the reverse transitions from each state in the DFA.

defs.name: minimize
defs.value: pub fn minimize(&Self) -> Dfa<T, &V> where T: Ord + Clone, V: Ord
defs.docs:
Minimize a DFA; i.e. find a DFA with the fewest states that is
 equivalent to the given DFA.
 Two DFAs are equivalent if, given the same string, they always lead to a
 state with the same associated value.

attributes.value: / Minimize a DFA; i.e. find a DFA with the fewest states that is
/ equivalent to the given DFA.
/ Two DFAs are equivalent if, given the same string, they always lead to a
/ state with the same associated value.

defs.name: equiv
defs.value:
pub fn equiv<U>(&Self, &Dfa<T, U>) -> bool where T: Ord + Clone, U: Ord,
V: Ord + PartialEq<U>
defs.docs: Compare DFAs by language equality.
